---
title: "Data analysis"
output: pdf_document
header-includes:
  - "\\usepackage{makecell}"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(afex)
library(emmeans)
library(kableExtra)

theme_set(theme_bw(base_size = 15) + theme(legend.position = "bottom"))
```

```{r}
# -----------------------------
# Load + prepare data
# -----------------------------
truth_ratings <- read_csv("ideal_counterbalanced.csv") %>%
  mutate(
    participant_id      = factor(participant_id),
    repetition_numeric  = as.numeric(repetition_times),
    repetition_factor   = factor(repetition_times, levels = c(0, 1, 9, 18, 27)),
    truth_score         = as.numeric(truth_score)  # keep numeric for means
  )

# -----------------------------
# 1) Age stats
# -----------------------------
age_summary <- truth_ratings %>%
  distinct(participant_id, age) %>%
  summarise(
    n = n(),
    mean_age = mean(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_age   = sd(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_age  = min(age, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_age  = max(age, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

age_summary

# -----------------------------
# Helper: within-subject d as described in paper footnote
# d = |m1 - m2| / sqrt(s1^2 + s2^2 - 2*r*s1*s2)
# where r is the correlation between paired measures.
# -----------------------------
d_within_r <- function(x, y) {
  x <- x[complete.cases(x, y)]
  y <- y[complete.cases(x, y)]
  m1 <- mean(x); m2 <- mean(y)
  s1 <- sd(x);   s2 <- sd(y)
  r  <- cor(x, y)
  denom <- sqrt(s1^2 + s2^2 - 2*r*s1*s2)
  abs(m1 - m2) / denom
}

# -----------------------------
# 2) Matched-pair t-test:
#    (a) repeated statements (collapsed across repetition>0)
#    (b) never-before-seen statements (repetition==0)
# -----------------------------
by_participant_cond <- truth_ratings %>%
  group_by(participant_id, repetition_numeric) %>%
  summarise(mean_truth = mean(truth_score, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

by_participant_repeated_vs_new <- by_participant_cond %>%
  mutate(item_type = if_else(repetition_numeric == 0, "new", "repeated")) %>%
  group_by(participant_id, item_type) %>%
  summarise(mean_truth = mean(mean_truth, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = item_type, values_from = mean_truth)

# Paired t-test (repeated vs new)
tt_repeated_vs_new <- t.test(
  by_participant_repeated_vs_new$repeated,
  by_participant_repeated_vs_new$new,
  paired = TRUE
)

tt_repeated_vs_new

# Effect size d in the paper’s style (within-subject using r / SDdiff closed form)
d_repeated_vs_new <- d_within_r(
  by_participant_repeated_vs_new$repeated,
  by_participant_repeated_vs_new$new
)

d_repeated_vs_new

# Report the Ms/SDs 
msd_repeated_new <- by_participant_repeated_vs_new %>%
  summarise(
    M_repeated = mean(repeated, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_repeated = sd(repeated, na.rm = TRUE),
    M_new = mean(new, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD_new = sd(new, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

msd_repeated_new

# -----------------------------
# 3) Correlations per participant:
#    mean truth ratings across repetition conditions vs
#    (a) repetitions (0,1,9,18,27)
#    (b) log(repetitions + 1)  [log1p]
# -----------------------------
# Create per-participant vector of condition means
wide_means <- by_participant_cond %>%
  mutate(rep_chr = as.character(repetition_numeric)) %>%
  select(participant_id, rep_chr, mean_truth) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = rep_chr, values_from = mean_truth)

rep_levels <- c("0","1","9","18","27")
rep_num <- as.numeric(rep_levels)
rep_log <- log(rep_num + 1)

# Compute r_linear and r_log for each participant using their 5 condition means
corrs <- wide_means %>%
  rowwise() %>%
  mutate(
    r_linear = {
      y <- c_across(all_of(rep_levels))
      if (anyNA(y)) NA_real_ else cor(y, rep_num)
    },
    r_log = {
      y <- c_across(all_of(rep_levels))
      if (anyNA(y)) NA_real_ else cor(y, rep_log)
    }
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

# Summary like paper (mean r, SD, range, % > 0)
corr_summary <- corrs %>%
  summarise(
    mean_r_linear = mean(r_linear, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_r_linear   = sd(r_linear, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_r_linear  = min(r_linear, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_r_linear  = max(r_linear, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_pos_linear = mean(r_linear > 0, na.rm = TRUE),
    
    mean_r_log = mean(r_log, na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_r_log   = sd(r_log, na.rm = TRUE),
    min_r_log  = min(r_log, na.rm = TRUE),
    max_r_log  = max(r_log, na.rm = TRUE),
    prop_pos_log = mean(r_log > 0, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

corr_summary

# Matched-pair t-test: is |r_log| greater than |r_linear|?
# (Paper compares magnitudes; if you prefer raw r, remove abs().)
tt_corr <- t.test(abs(corrs$r_log), abs(corrs$r_linear), paired = TRUE)
tt_corr

# Paired dz for this comparison (common reporting for paired t)
diff_r <- abs(corrs$r_log) - abs(corrs$r_linear)
d_dz_corr <- mean(diff_r, na.rm = TRUE) / sd(diff_r, na.rm = TRUE)
d_dz_corr

# -----------------------------
# 4) RM ANOVA and Bonferroni-adjusted pairwise comparisons
# -----------------------------
afex::afex_options(type = 3)

aov_rm <- aov_car(
  truth_score ~ repetition_factor + Error(participant_id/repetition_factor),
  data = truth_ratings,
  factorize = FALSE
)

aov_rm

emm_rep <- emmeans(aov_rm, ~ repetition_factor)

pairs_bonf <- pairs(emm_rep, adjust = "bonferroni") %>%
  summary(infer = TRUE) %>%
  as.data.frame()

pairs_bonf

# -----------------------------
# 5) Table 2-style output:
#    - row 1: M and SD per condition
#    - lower triangle: d values (paper-style within-subject d)
#    - star for Bonferroni p < .05
# -----------------------------

# Condition-level descriptives (Session 2 truth ratings by repetition condition)
desc_by_cond <- truth_ratings %>%
  group_by(repetition_factor) %>%
  summarise(
    M = mean(truth_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    SD = sd(truth_score, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(repetition_factor = as.character(repetition_factor))

desc_by_cond

# Function to fetch Bonferroni p for pair a vs b from emmeans contrasts
get_p_bonf <- function(a, b, pairs_df) {
  c1 <- paste(a, "-", b)
  c2 <- paste(b, "-", a)
  p <- pairs_df$p.value[pairs_df$contrast == c1]
  if (length(p) == 0) p <- pairs_df$p.value[pairs_df$contrast == c2]
  if (length(p) == 0) return(NA_real_)
  p
}

# Build wide participant means (already have wide_means), ensure columns exist
# wide_means columns are "0","1","9","18","27" as characters
stopifnot(all(rep_levels %in% names(wide_means)))

# Build triangular matrix of formatted cells
cell_matrix <- matrix("",
                      nrow = length(rep_levels),
                      ncol = length(rep_levels),
                      dimnames = list(rep_levels, rep_levels)
)

for (i in seq_along(rep_levels)) {
  for (j in seq_along(rep_levels)) {

    if (i == j) {
      cell_matrix[i, j] <- "–"

    } else if (i < j) {

      a <- rep_levels[i]
      b <- rep_levels[j]

      # compute effect size
      d_val <- d_within_r(wide_means[[a]], wide_means[[b]])

      # get Bonferroni p-value
      p_val <- get_p_bonf(a, b, pairs_bonf)

      # format value
      d_text <- sprintf("d = %.2f", d_val)

      # add star if significant
      if (!is.na(p_val) && p_val < 0.05) {
        d_text <- paste0(d_text, "*")
      }

      # bold if significant (LaTeX)
      if (!is.na(p_val) && p_val < 0.05) {
        d_text <- sprintf("\\textbf{%s}", d_text)
      }

      cell_matrix[i, j] <- d_text
    }
  }
}



d_table_formatted <- as.data.frame(cell_matrix)

# Build header row for Ms and SDs like paper (as a 1-row table)
msd_header <- desc_by_cond %>%
  mutate(
    msd = sprintf("\\makecell{M = %.2f \\\\ SD = %.2f}", M, SD)
  ) %>%
  select(repetition_factor, msd) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = repetition_factor, values_from = msd) %>%
  as.data.frame()


# Combine into a single display table:
# First row: Ms/SDs
# Then the d-matrix (with row labels)
final_table <- bind_rows(
  tibble(` ` = "Ms and SDs of truth ratings") %>% bind_cols(msd_header),
  tibble(` ` = rownames(d_table_formatted)) %>% bind_cols(d_table_formatted)
)

# Print for PDF knitting (escape=FALSE to allow LaTeX bold)
knitr::kable(final_table, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE, escape = FALSE, align = "lccccc") %>%
  kable_styling(latex_options = c("hold_position")) %>%
  add_header_above(c(" " = 1, "Number of Session 1 repetitions" = 5))
```
